source_jobs.title: Special Projects Lead (India-Based)
source_jobs.description:
JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.

The Opportunity

We are looking for someone who wants to work on a problem that matters. Not optimize a funnel. Not manage a pipeline. Work on a problem that actually matters — how AI gets adopted inside law firms and legal teams at scale, and what it takes to make that change stick.

The Special Projects Lead sits at the intersection of growth, strategy, and deployment. You will own the entire motion from first conversation to live workflows — helping clients understand what is possible, demonstrating the product, driving the deal, and staying accountable to outcomes long after the contract is signed. This is not a role for someone who wants to hand off and move on.

You will work directly with the founders, operate across every function — sales, customer success, and go-to-market — and shape the playbook for how August expands across India and beyond. The team is expanding massively, and the people who join now will define how we operate at scale. If you are energized by complexity, thrive in ambiguous environments, and want to see your work change how a profession operates, this role is for you.



What You'll Do

 - Meet with partners, senior counsel, and in-house legal teams to understand their most critical workflows and where they're losing time

 - Lead product demonstrations and translate what you're seeing in the field into compelling cases for adoption

 - Own deals end-to-end — from first conversation through commercial close

 - Drive customer success post-deployment, ensuring the product creates real impact on day-to-day legal operations

 - Lead training sessions, workflow design, and ongoing check-ins as clients expand to new practice groups and teams

 - Represent August at conferences, industry events, and client sites across India (significant travel required)

 - Work with the product and engineering teams to shape how field insights get built into August

 - Scope new client opportunities across practice areas, firm sizes, and geographies



What You'll Bring

 - Significant experience at a tier 1 Indian law firm, ideally exceeding 4 years of post-qualification experience

 - A track record of navigating complex, multi-stakeholder relationships and driving decisions through to outcomes

 - Strong commercial instincts — you can quickly understand a client's business, identify the highest-leverage opportunities, and build a clear path forward

 - Comfort working without a defined playbook and the judgment to build one from scratch

 - The ability to communicate clearly across audiences — from associates to managing partners to in-house leadership

 - Willingness and enthusiasm for significant travel



Nice to Have

Prior exposure to legal operations, legal tech, or professional services. JD or legal industry background a plus.



Location

Remote (India).





WHY JOIN AUGUST

 - Founding Impact: Shape not just your r

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for an Account Executive who is excited to own our sales motion from the ground up. You’ll work directly with the founders, engage some of the most forward-looking law firms, and help shape how AI is adopted across the legal industry.

You’ll need to be comfortable leading complex sales cycles, deeply understanding customer workflows, and translating technical capabilities into business value. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment, loves building new markets, and is energized by high ownership.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the full sales motion. Run deals from cold to close across our entire customer base — law firms and in-house legal teams alike.

 - Build the playbook. Discovery scripts, demo flow, objection library, pricing posture, deal stages — none of this exists yet. You build it, document it, and refine it as you sell.

 - Sell outcomes, not tools. Structure deals as fixed-fee engagements, not seat-based subscriptions. Help buyers reframe how they procure legal work.

 - Partner with product and engineering. Every lost deal becomes a product input. Every won deal becomes a workflow we encode. You are the connective tissue.

 - Represent August in the field. Practice-specific conferences are now our primary pipeline source. Show up, speak, write, and become the August face the market recognizes.

 - Use AI to outperform. Operate with the full stack — Clay, Apollo, Bird Dog, intent tools, custom AI agents. We do not hire AEs who sell the old way at a company that sells the new way.




ABOUT YOU

 - 3–6 years of experience in a closing sales role (SaaS, Legal Tech, or B2B Enterprise preferred).

 - Track record of consistently exceeding quota and owning complex, multi-threaded sales.

 - Strong consultative sales skills — you can dig into a prospect’s problems, not just pitch features.

 - Comfort navigating technical conversations and translating value for non-technical buyers.

 - Ability to thrive in ambiguity and adapt quickly as we scale.

 - Bonus: Experience selling into legal, professional services, or highly regulated industries.

 - Bonus: JD or prior legal industry experience.




BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Centralized office equipment ordering for all employees

 - Equinox or Chelsea Piers gym membership

 - In-office perks: lunch, dinner, snacks, drinks, and more

 - Budget for intra-office travel

 - Team-building events

 - Relocation support to NYC (as needed)




WHY JOIN AUGUST

 - Founding Impact: Shape not just your role but the company.

 - Uncapped Upside: Competitive base + commission, early equity ownership.

 - Top-tier Team: Work alongside people who move fast, think clearly, and care deeply.

 - Category-Defining Work: Help build the first true AI agents for the legal profession.

 - Fast Growth: Scale you

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re building a generational company at August, and we’re looking for a Growth Marketing Manager who builds faster than most teams can write specs.

In this role, you’ll own August’s growth infrastructure end to end. You’ll connect our GTM stack, automate workflows, prototype and ship AI-powered experiments, build landing pages, and invent new ways to reach lawyers. You’ll use LLMs, no-code and low-code tools, APIs, and scrappy prototypes to unlock new growth channels and scale what works.

You’ll be embedded within the growth function, working across paid, lifecycle, outbound, and product-led growth. Your toolkit will be distinct: GPTs, automation workflows, custom scripts, internal agents, and fast MVPs built to learn, not to impress. This is a high-autonomy, high-leverage role for someone who thinks in distribution, speed, and scale. You think like a growth marketer but move like a hacker.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own our GTM stack: Connect and optimize tools across marketing and sales to power outbound, inbound, and PLG motion

 - Build AI-powered workflows: Prototype and launch GPTs for ad copy, agents for segmentation, auto-generated landing pages, and smart automation across channels

 - Ship growth experiments fast: Build and launch MVPs using Retool, Bubble, Zapier, Vercel, or custom scripts without relying on Engineering

 - Automate repetitive work: Campaign QA, performance reporting, lead enrichment, data pipelines—if it’s manual, make it automatic

 - Build and scale performance channels: Paid search, retargeting, SEO, and new experiments we haven’t tried yet. Obsess over ROI and repeatable systems

 - Support PLG motion: Build landing pages, improve site speed, optimize conversion funnels, and run keyword research

 - Leverage APIs, webhooks, and LLMs: Stitch together growth infrastructure that scales smarter, not harder

 - Run outbound campaigns: Build and optimize systems that convert, from segmentation to sequencing

 - Act as an internal multiplier: Share tools, playbooks, and internal agents that help marketing and GTM teams move faster

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Work closely with design, product, and data science to test and refine ideas that unlock new growth levers


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Highly interested in: growth, GTM engineering, marketing ops, or similar roles at fast-growing B2B SaaS companies

 - Growth instincts + hacker mindset: You know what drives outcomes and how to build just enough to prove value

 - Technical fluency: Comfortable with Zapier/Make/Tray/N8N, Python, JavaScript, SQL, APIs, webhooks, and no-code tools like Retool or Framer

 - AI-first approach: Hands-on experience with LLMs, automation, and experimentation. Bonus if you’ve built custom GPTs or internal agents

 - Modern GTM tooling knowledge: Familiar with systems like HubSpot, Segment, Amplitude, Clay, Saleslof

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.




THE OPPORTUNITY

August is building AGI for law. Our agents mimic a lawyers’ reasoning, research, and drafting, and our workflows already outperform lawyers in third-party evaluations. We’re targeting mid-market law firms, automating their legal work and allowing them to provide our tools directly to their clients. Join August at an early stage as we scale our agents to process thousands of documents simultaneously and create outputs exceeding human-level accuracy.

As a Founding Designer at August, you’ll own the design function end-to-end—shaping the product experience, refining our brand identity, and setting the design culture. You’ll work closely with the founders, engineers, and legal experts to craft intuitive interfaces for complex AI workflows and create a brand that resonates with law firms worldwide




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead design for product and brand—owning UI/UX, visual identity, and marketing materials.

 - Translate complex legal AI workflows into simple, intuitive user experiences.

 - Partner with engineers to prototype, test, and ship AI-native interfaces at speed.

 - Develop a consistent visual language across product, website, and external communications.

 - Help build the foundation of August’s design culture, processes, and standards.




ABOUT YOU

 - 2+ years of professional design experience (product, brand, or both).

 - Strong portfolio showcasing polished product design and/or brand systems.

 - Proficiency with modern design tools (Figma or equivalent) and comfort with rapid iteration.

 - Ability to design elegant solutions for complex workflows.

 - Comfortable working at an early stage company with ownership and autonomy.




BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Parental Leave

 - Ce

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.




THE ROLE: LEGAL PRODUCT CHAMPION

This role doesn’t sit neatly in a box. It’s part lawyer, part product thinker, part trusted partner to our clients. As a Legal Product Champion, you’ll help midsized law firms adopt August, guide them through change, and show them how AI can be a real competitive edge.

You’ll be the lawyer’s voice inside August — translating practice needs into product insights, influencing roadmap priorities, and helping us build tools lawyers actually use.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner with firms: Build trust with partners, associates, and innovation teams; advise them on where AI fits into their workflows.

 - Drive adoption: Lead pilots, onboarding, and rollouts across practice groups and firms.

 - Translate practice into product: Capture lawyer pain points and opportunities, feeding directly into product and engineering conversations.

 - Create scalable playbooks: Develop training, resources, and case studies that make adoption smoother across clients.

 - Show impact: Help firms measure ROI and highlight wins — from faster drafting to better client service.

 - Shape the role: This is a new function at August. You’ll help define what “Legal Product” means here as we scale.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - JD required, with 2–6 years practicing law at a law firm (BigLaw or leading midsized).

 - Deep understanding of law firm workflows (litigation or corporate).

 - A curiosity about AI and technology and how they can reshape practice.

 - Strong communicator: credible with senior partners, relatable to associates, clear with engineers.

 - Comfort navigating ambiguous, fast-moving environments.

 - A builder’s mindset — proactive, hands-on, and focused on impact over perfection.




BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 -

JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a software engineer to build and optimize the technical infrastructure that powers customer acquisition. You'll design data pipelines, integrate third-party APIs, build experiment frameworks, and systematically improve performance across our outbound, SEO, and automation systems.

This is systems engineering applied to go-to-market. You'll write code, design architectures, instrument analytics, and run optimization experiments the same technical work as any backend or infra engineer, just applied to acquisition systems instead of product features.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


OUTBOUND AUTOMATION SYSTEMS

 - Design and implement multi-channel prospecting pipelines using Clay, Amplemarket, and Apollo APIs

 - Build experiment frameworks to A/B test email sequences, subject lines, send times, and message variants

 - Develop lead enrichment and scoring systems with automated data quality checks

 - Implement webhook-based response detection and automated routing logic

 - Optimize deliverability: SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration, domain warmup automation, bounce handling


LINKEDIN AUTOMATION INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Build LinkedIn prospecting automation: connection requests, message sequences, engagement tracking

 - Design rate limiting and anti-detection systems to maintain account health at scale

 - Implement targeting optimization: systematic testing of job titles, seniority, industries, company sizes

 - Build analytics pipeline to track connection rates, response rates, meeting bookings by segment

 - Develop session management and proxy rotation infrastructure


SEO INFRASTRUCTURE & OPTIMIZATION

 - Automate technical SEO: schema markup generation, sitemap updates, crawl error monitoring

 - Build content optimization pipelines:

THE ROLE

Build and lead August's presence across the tech and AI ecosystem. You'll design and host events (dinners, conferences, panels) that bring together lawyers interested in technology, engineers, product leaders, and AI enthusiasts who care about the future of professional services.

This is proactive, strategic, and outbound-driven. You'll identify who matters across the broader tech and AI world (not just legal tech) and turn those relationships into high-value gatherings that position August as a leader in applied AI.

You'll own the entire event lifecycle and serve as August's connector to the broader tech and AI ecosystem: building relationships with press, analysts, and industry leaders that amplify our brand.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


EVENTS & COMMUNITY:

 - Design and execute multi-format events:
   
   - Intimate dinners (10-15): Managing partners, CIOs, tech leaders
   
   - Conference presence: ABA TECHSHOW, ILTACON, Legalweek, ReInvent Law
   
   - Roundtables (15-30): practice-area specific
   
   - Customer Advisory Panels: quarterly product roadmap sessions
   
   - Virtual sessions: webinars, fireside chats, AMAs
   
   - Workshops: AI training for legal professionals

 - Build a network across the tech and AI ecosystem:
   
   - Lawyers interested in technology and automation
   
   - Managing partners and CIOs at professional services firms
   
   - Tech founders, product leaders, and engineers
   
   - Innovation directors and COOs exploring AI
   
   - AI researchers, policy experts, journalists

 - Execute proactive outbound: LinkedIn, email campaigns, warm intros, conference targeting


REVENUE & PARTNERSHIPS:

 - Treat events as a revenue channel: track in HubSpot, measure pipeline influence, calculate ROI

source_jobs.title: AI Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As an AI Deployment Strategist, you will be the person who walks into a law firm and actually changes how it operates. You will embed with clients, earn trust at every level of the organization, identify where AI can replace manual workflows, and own the transformation from discovery through full adoption. This is not a coordination role — you are in the field, running sessions, building solutions, and making things happen.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Embed with client organizations and run structured AI discovery across teams and practice groups — mapping workflows, uncovering pain points, and identifying where time is being wasted.

 - Conduct one-on-one sessions with lawyers, paralegals, and legal ops staff to find out what they wish AI could do for them — going deeper than demos to surface the real opportunities.

 - Run generalist AI training sessions across user groups, showing people what’s possible and helping them rethink how their work gets done.

 - Map and automate legal workflows — document review, form filling, intake, drafting, redlining, matter management — and build lightweight proofs-of-concept to demonstrate value quickly.

 - Own the go-live for AI solutions within client organizations from kickoff through full adoption, coordinating technical resources on both sides.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for client stakeholders throughout the engagement, running structured check-ins and business reviews.

 - Translate what you learn in the field directly into product feedback for August’s engineering team.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong product sense — you understand what makes a workflow painful and what would actually fix it, even without deep engineering knowledge.

 - Business acumen and interpersonal range — you can earn trust with a managing partner and a legal ops analyst in the same afternoon.

 - Natural curiosity — you go deep in conversations, ask unexpected questions, and find the real problem underneath the stated one.

 - Comfort with ambiguity — there is no playbook here; you will be helping write it.

 - 1–3 years of experience in consulting, legal ops, client success, product, or a client-facing role at a tech company. Background in law or legal services is a plus.

 - Familiarity with workflow automation or no-code tools (Zapier, Make, n8n, or similar) is helpful but not required.

 - Excitement about working in person at our NYC office with a team that values precision, pace, and constant reinvention.




BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Parental Leave

source_jobs.title: Strategy and Operations
source_jobs.description:
JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.



About the Role:

As a Strategy & Operations Lead, you will be the operating spine of August as we scale. You will turn strategic priorities into shipped systems, build out the partnerships channel from the ground up, and run the discrete projects that don't fit anywhere else. This is not a coordination role — you report directly to the founders, take ambiguous priorities, decide what to do about them, and own the outcome.



What you'll do:

 - Build and own August's operating cadence — the weekly product-to-GTM digest, the sales-to-product loop, the monthly metrics review, and the dashboards that make all of it self-running as we scale.

 - Source, structure, and close commercial partnerships across two audiences. Integration partners that embed August in the legal tech stack, typically through rev-share negotiations that need to be closed before any technical work ships. Distribution partners that route legal work toward us — including industry consultants, bar associations, insurance carriers and brokers, and industry bodies.

 - Run two-week strategic sprints on whatever is highest-leverage that month — pricing for new practice areas, jurisdiction expansion, scoping new partnership profiles, modeling new sales motions.

 - Partner with product, engineering, GTM, and the legal team to take ambiguous founder priorities, build what needs to exist, and hand finished systems to long-term owners.

 - Track and report on partnership performance and operational metrics, surfacing insights that drive decisions in real time.



What we're looking for:

 - 2–4 years of experience in strategy consulting, operations, BizOps, finance, or a high-performance startup in a Strategy & Ops or Chief-of-Staff seat. Priority given to former founders and people who can show us things they have shipped.

 - Demonstrated ability to take an ambiguous brief and run it without supervision. You see things falling on the floor and pick them up before being asked.

 - AI-native operating fluency. AI is not a tool you sometimes use; it is how you operate. You can show us automations, agents, or internal tools you have built for yourself — not just used.

 - Comfort with commercial deal structure — enough to hold your own in a rev-share or partnership negotiation.

 - Strong written and verbal communication. You can synthesize messy input into a clear plan and defend it when challenged.

 - Excitement about working in person at our NYC office, with a team that values precision, pace, and constant reinvention.




BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Centralized office equipment ordering for all employees

 - Equinox or Chelsea Piers gym membership

 - In-office perks: lunch, dinner, snacks, drinks, and more

 - Budget for intra-office travel

 - Team-building events

 - Relocation support to NYC (as needed)


WHY JOIN AUGUST

 - Founding Imp

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
JOIN US IN BUILDING THE AUTOPILOT FOR LEGAL SERVICES

Today, legal work is done the way it has been done for a century — manually, by humans, in six-minute increments. Every contract drafted from scratch. Every memo researched line by line. Every matter running through layers of review that someone is paying for at hundreds of dollars an hour.

That is about to change. We believe that in five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before it ever reaches a human. The implication is not faster lawyers — it is a fundamentally different way that legal services get bought and procured.

Our mission is to make high-quality legal work accessible and affordable for everyone. We are going after the services spend, not the software spend — helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI, and helping in-house teams do the same. One engine of agents and workflows underneath every matter we touch.

We are a lean, in-person based in New York City, backed by leading investors and growing fast. Every role at August is a chance to remake an industry while the window is open.


THE OPPORTUNITY

August is building the world's largest AI-native legal services company — and rewriting how law firms scale. Our agents mimic a lawyers’ reasoning, research, and drafting, and our workflows already outperform lawyers in third-party evaluations. We’re targeting mid-market law firms, automating their legal work and allowing them to provide our tools directly to their clients. Join August at an early stage as we scale our agents to process thousands of documents simultaneously and create outputs exceeding human-level accuracy.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own deployments end-to-end: architect, build, and ship August into production at law firms — scope the integration, write the code, run the cutover, and stay on it until it's stable. You decide what "done" means.

 - Build the integrations that touch everything: ship the adapters, webhooks, and APIs that wire August into iManage, NetDocs, SharePoint, Outlook, and Word add-ins; implement SSO/SAML/OIDC, tenant isolation, and ingestion pipelines that hold up at enterprise scale.

 - Turn one-off work into platform: every deployment is raw material — extract the patterns, harden them into configurable modules, SDKs, and internal tooling, and make the next firm onboard in half the time.

 - Debug live, in production, under pressure: when a migration stalls, a policy blocks a write, or a legacy system returns garbage, you trace it, root-cause it, and ship the fix the same day. Instrument everything so the next deployment doesn't hit the same wall.

 


ABOUT YOU

 - 3+ years of production software engineering experience (full-stack or platform); you're a strong generalist who can navigate an unfamiliar codebase quickly, debug hairy integration issues, and ship reliable code under real constraints.

 - Strong command of TypeScript/JavaScript (React/Next.js) and at least one backend language (Python preferred); comfortable writing API clients, data transformation scripts, and lightweight services from scratch.

 - Engineering instincts that favor shipping: you default to the smallest working solution, add observability from day one, and treat each deployment as a learning loop — not a one-time project.

 - Thrives in high-ownership, fast-moving environments: you're energized by ambiguity, can context-switch between deep technical problems and high-level architecture discussions, and take pride in building things that actually work in the wild.

 


BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Parental Leave

 - Centralized office equipment ordering for all employees

 - Equinox or Chelsea Piers gym membership

 - In-office perks: lunch, dinner, snacks, drinks, and more

 - Budget for intra-office travel

 - Team-building events

 - Relocation support to NYC (as needed)

 


WHY JOIN AUGUST

 - Founding Impact: Shape not just your role but the company.

 - Uncapped Upsid

We’re targeting mid-market law firms, automating their legal work and allowing them to provide our tools directly to their clients. Join August at an early stage as we scale our agents to process thousands of documents simultaneously and create outputs exceeding human-level accuracy.

As a Full-Stack Engineer at August, you will design and scale production-grade agents that perform legal tasks with expert-level reasoning. You’ll work closely with the founders, legal experts, and product team to deploy AI systems in high-stakes workflows used by real lawyers across the globe.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build intuitive and optimized UI/UX using React and TypeScript for workflows that integrate reasoning LLMs and agentic architectures.

 - Zero-to-one product development: rapidly prototype, evaluate, integrate, and test new product features in close partnership with our customers and legal team.

 - Develop new AI native workflows: implement streaming, long-running tasks, procedural UX, etc. for new AI tasks, finding the balance between state-of-the-art and pragmatism.




ABOUT YOU

 - 2+ years experience building production-quality frontend applications.

 - Proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript, React, and Next.js.

 - Proven ability to create polished, intuitive user interfaces with attention to detail.

 - Comfortable working at an early stage company with ownership and rapid iteration.




BENEFITS

 - Medical insurance coverage

 - Flexible PTO

 - Parental Leave

 - Centralized office equipment ordering for all employees

 - Equinox or Chelsea Piers gym membership

 - In-office perks: lunch, dinner, snacks, drinks, and more

 - Budget for intra-office travel

 - Team-building events

 - Relocation support to NYC (as needed)




WHY JOIN AUGUST

 - Founding Impact: Shape not just your role but the company.

 - Uncapped Upside: Competitive base + commission, early equity ownership.

 - Top-tier Team: Work alongside people who move fast, think clearly, and care deeply.

 - Category-Defining Work: Help build the first true AI agents for the legal profession.

 - Fast Growth: Scale your career as we scale the company.

 - In-person in NYC: Collaborative, talent dense and high energy environment

 - Global Reach: Our clients span 4 continents and use August in multiple languages and jurisdictions

 - Frontier of Applied AI: August has access to proprietary data inaccessible to foundation model providers. We build agents to automate all challenging knowledge work.
